title: Greek Yogurt Recipe: High Protein Parfait
metaDescription: Greek yogurt parfait with 26g of protein per serving — layered with granola and fresh berries. Ready in 5 minutes, no cooking required.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: greek yogurt parfait, high protein breakfast, greek yogurt recipe
ingredients: 1/4 tsp vanilla extract (optional, stirred into yogurt)

steps.name: Mix the yogurt
steps.text: Stir vanilla extract into the Greek yogurt, if using.

steps.name: Layer
steps.text: In a glass, layer a third of the yogurt, a third of the granola, and a third of the berries. Repeat twice more.

steps.name: Finish
steps.text: Drizzle honey over the top layer and serve immediately so the granola stays crisp.

tips.question: Why does my parfait get soggy?
tips.answer: Granola softens fast once it touches yogurt — assemble right before eating rather than prepping it hours ahead if you want the crunch to hold.

tips.question: Can I meal-prep this for the week?
tips.answer: Store the yogurt, granola, and berries separately in the fridge and layer them fresh each morning — combined, it only holds up well for about 30 minutes.

tips.question: What Greek yogurt has the most protein?
tips.answer: Plain, unsweetened Greek yogurt has roughly double the protein of flavored varieties, since flavored versions trade some yogurt for added sugar and thickeners.

tips.question: Can I use regular yogurt instead of Greek yogurt?
tips.answer: You can, but regular yogurt has about half the protein and a thinner texture — the parfait won't hold its layers as cleanly.

storyIntro: A parfait is really just an argument for eating breakfast in layers instead of a bowl — Greek yogurt, granola, and berries stacked so every bite gets a bit of all three instead of one flavor dominating.
Plain Greek yogurt is doing the heavy lifting here at 26g of protein per serving, with granola and honey adding just enough sweetness that it still feels like a treat.
storyNote: Assemble it right before eating, not the night before — granola loses its crunch fast once it's sitting in yogurt, and that crunch is half the point of a parfait.
